name: Farley
coordsSource: web-verified:genuki SU225296;historic-england farley-primary-school SU224295 36m-agree;wikipedia-grid SU225293 rejected-300m-south-outlier; 2026-06-05
overview: Farley is a village in south Wiltshire, lying about 7 miles east of Salisbury just off the A30 London Road and forming the joint civil and ecclesiastical parish of Pitton and Farley with neighbouring Pitton a mile and a half to the north. Its outstanding landmark is the Grade I listed All Saints' Church, built in the 1680s under the patronage of Sir Stephen Fox with strong design influence from Sir Christopher Wren, alongside the contemporary Grade I listed Farley Hospital almshouses. Farley sits on clay (while Pitton sits on chalk) in a quiet downland valley east of Clarendon.
character: A small, historic and tight-knit downland village of brick and flint cottages, known for its remarkable Wren-influenced church and a lively annual music and literary festival.
amenities: The Hook and Glove village pub
Farley village hall and Farley Nursery School
Shop and post office and further pub (Silver Plough) in neighbouring Pitton

commutes.notes: About 7 miles west via the A30 London Road
Nearest mainline station at Salisbury (London Waterloo to Exeter line); Grateley also accessible to the north-east

prices.summary: Mainly period cottages and detached village houses; properties in this SP5 area east of Salisbury typically sell from around the mid hundreds of thousands of pounds upward, with larger homes commanding more.

thingsToDo: Farley Music Festival each June and the Farley Literary Festival
Visiting the Wren-influenced All Saints' Church and Farley almshouses
Walking and riding around Clarendon, Bentley Wood and the surrounding downland
placesToEat: The Hook and Glove, Farley (village pub)
The Silver Plough, Pitton (well-known nearby dining pub)
pros: Exceptional Grade I listed church and historic almshouses
Strong village community with festivals and events
Quiet downland setting within easy reach of Salisbury and the A30
cons: No railway station in the village; reliance on Salisbury or Grateley
Limited shopping, with the main shop in neighbouring Pitton
Nearest primary school is a few miles away
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a historic, community-minded Wiltshire village within commuting distance of Salisbury who appreciate heritage and the arts.
